tiktok.caption: The 30% rent rule is from 1969 — written for $8,400 salaries. Here's why following it today is a mistake.

youtube_short.title: The 30% Rent Rule Is From 1969
title_alternatives: This Rent Rule Was Never Financial Advice
Nobody Told You Where the 30% Rent Rule Came From
youtube_short.description: The '30% of income on rent' rule is quoted by every finance guru — but it came from a 1969 HUD housing policy designed to identify who qualified for government assistance, not to guide everyday financial decisions. To actually follow it today you'd need to earn $82,000 a year. The median US income is $56,000. The metric that actually matters is your savings rate — if you're saving 15-20% every month, your rent percentage is just noise. Follow for more finance myths debunked daily. #personalfinance #moneymyths #savingsrate #rentinglife
